source: Generated 2026-07-03 by argon2-cffi 25.1.0 low_level.hash_secret_raw(type=Type.ID, version=0x13) — bindings to the P-H-C reference Argon2 library, an implementation independent of both the C99 module and libsodium; oracle sanity-checked before minting: it reproduces byte-for-byte all 4 KATs pinned in src/main.cpp test-argon2id-c99 that were originally generated by libsodium crypto_pwhash_argon2id (so vectors 0-3 are dual-oracle: libsodium-generated pin + argon2-cffi recomputation, SAME bytes as the binary pins); vectors 4-10 are fresh argon2-cffi-generated cases across the parameter surface the shipped API supports (t in 1..4, m in 8..1024 KiB including the m=8*p floor, p in 1..4 exercising the sequential cross-lane path, salt lengths 8/16, password lengths 0/25..29/32/64, tag lengths 16/32/64); the RFC 9106 section 5.3 published vector is DELIBERATELY ABSENT: it binds the optional secret K (8x0x03) and associated-data X (12x0x04), which determ_argon2id fixes at length 0 (include/determ/crypto/argon2/argon2id.h + src/crypto/argon2/README.md section 5, matching libsodium's crypto_pwhash_argon2id surface), so no in-repo consumer can compute it — vector 4 instead pins the section 5.3 COST parameters (t=3, m=32 KiB, p=4, pwd=32x0x01, salt=16x0x02, taglen=32) with K/X empty, whose tag is oracle-generated and intentionally differs from the RFC's published tag

_meta.oracle: argon2-cffi 25.1.0 (pip, P-H-C reference bindings), argon2.low_level.hash_secret_raw(secret=password, salt=salt, time_cost=t_cost, memory_cost=m_cost_kib, parallelism=parallelism, hash_len=outlen, type=Type.ID, version=19)

sourcing.vectors 0-3: dual-oracle: byte-identical to the libsodium crypto_pwhash_argon2id KATs pinned in src/main.cpp test-argon2id-c99, independently recomputed by argon2-cffi at generation time
sourcing.vectors 4-10: oracle-verified: freshly generated by argon2-cffi (no second oracle run, but the same oracle instance matched libsodium on vectors 0-3 first)
sourcing.rfc_transcribed: none — RFC 9106 section 5.3 requires secret+AD inputs the shipped determ_argon2id API does not expose (fixed at length 0)

_meta.api_mapping: determ_argon2id(out, outlen, pwd, pwdlen, salt, saltlen, t_cost, m_cost, parallelism) with m_cost in KiB, Argon2id v1.3 — argument order per include/determ/crypto/argon2/argon2id.h
_meta.runner_note: tools/test_c99_vector_files.sh fail-closes on primitives without a python recomputation checker: this file needs a chk_argon2id checker (argon2-cffi based) + an EXPECTED entry added to that runner before/with landing, or the vector-file gate turns RED (same wiring step the frost corpus went through)

vectors.name: t=1 m=8KiB p=1 outlen=32 - pinned in src/main.cpp test-argon2id-c99 (libsodium-sourced, argon2-cffi re-verified)
t=3 m=32KiB p=1 outlen=32 - pinned in src/main.cpp test-argon2id-c99 (libsodium-sourced, argon2-cffi re-verified)
t=2 m=256KiB p=1 outlen=64 - pinned in src/main.cpp test-argon2id-c99 (libsodium-sourced, argon2-cffi re-verified)
t=4 m=1MiB p=1 outlen=32 - pinned in src/main.cpp test-argon2id-c99 (libsodium-sourced, argon2-cffi re-verified)

RFC 9106 section 5.3 COST parameters (t=3 m=32KiB p=4, pwd=32x0x01, salt=16x0x02) with secret/AD empty - oracle-generated tag, intentionally NOT the RFC's published tag (which binds K=8x0x03, X=12x0x04 that the shipped API cannot supply)
p=2 cross-lane, taglen=64: t=1 m=16KiB p=2 outlen=64
p=3 odd lanes at the m=8*p floor: t=2 m=24KiB p=3 outlen=32
spec-minimum 8-byte salt: t=2 m=32KiB p=1 outlen=32
64-byte password (one BLAKE2b block): t=1 m=32KiB p=1 outlen=32
empty password: t=1 m=8KiB p=1 outlen=32
